differential_diagnoses: Common variable immunodeficiency (CVID) with associated enteropathy
next_steps: Surgical consultation for management of jejunal aneurysmal dilation and suspected perforation risk
Double-balloon enteroscopy with deep biopsy of the suspicious jejunal segments
Immunophenotyping of intestinal biopsy specimens (CD3, CD8, CD4, CD56, TIA-1, granzyme B, EBER)
T-cell receptor (TCR) gene rearrangement studies on intestinal tissue
CT-guided biopsy or exploratory laparotomy if endoscopic access is insufficient for definitive diagnosis
Repeat serum immunoglobulin quantification and lymphocyte subset analysis
Bone marrow biopsy and aspirate to evaluate for systemic involvement of lymphoid malignancy
